questions.question: GRADUALLY
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Gradually = धीरे-धीरे
  • Opposite Idea: Abruptly = अचानक
  • Example: The temperature rose gradually, not abruptly.
  • Exam Tip: Words showing sudden change are usually antonyms of gradually.

questions.question: CRUCIAL
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Crucial = बहुत महत्वपूर्ण
  • Opposite Idea: Trivial = तुच्छ / मामूली
  • Example: This decision is crucial, not trivial.
  • Exam Tip: Important ↔ Unimportant is a common antonym pair.

questions.question: ENDORSE
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Endorse = समर्थन करना
  • Opposite Idea: Renounce = त्याग देना
  • Example: He endorsed the plan instead of renouncing it.
  • Exam Tip: Approve ↔ Reject is a frequent SSC pattern.

questions.question: TEMPORARY
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Temporary = अस्थायी
  • Opposite Idea: Lasting = स्थायी
  • Example: Pain is temporary, but success is lasting.
  • Exam Tip: Short-term ↔ Permanent is very common.

questions.question: ABOMINABLE
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Abominable = बहुत घृणित
  • Opposite Idea: Delightful = आनंददायक
  • Example: The weather was delightful, not abominable.
  • Exam Tip: Extreme negative ↔ extreme positive words.

questions.question: BLOATED
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Bloated = फूला हुआ
  • Opposite Idea: Deflated = पिचका हुआ
  • Example: The balloon became deflated after being bloated.
  • Exam Tip: Swollen ↔ Shrunk type pairs are common.

questions.question: DEVOUT
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Devout = अत्यंत समर्पित
  • Opposite Idea: Passive = निष्क्रिय
  • Example: He is devout in faith, not passive.
  • Exam Tip: Active belief ↔ Lack of involvement.

questions.question: MANIFEST
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Manifest = स्पष्ट
  • Opposite Idea: Disguise = छिपाना
  • Example: His fear was disguised, not manifest.
  • Exam Tip: Visible ↔ Hidden is a high-frequency pair.

questions.question: PERSEVERANCE
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Perseverance = धैर्यपूर्वक प्रयास
  • Opposite Idea: Instability = अस्थिरता
  • Example: Success needs perseverance, not instability.
  • Exam Tip: Stability ↔ Instability is often tested.
